fractions_decimals.questionBridge: Simon slides the ledger toward you. Break the whole into parts. Every tenth counts.

ratios_proportions.settingLine: Giza, foot of the pyramid, 600 BC
ratios_proportions.questionBridge: Thales measures the shadow against his staff. If one thing scales, so does the other.

order_of_operations.questionBridge: Ada taps the punch cards. Do the steps in the right order. The machine forgives nothing.

basic_equations.questionBridge: Diophantus scratches a riddle into the wax. A number is hiding. Undo each step until you find it.

linear_equations.questionBridge: William stares at the balance. One unknown. Find what makes both sides equal.

functions_basics.questionBridge: Euler scrawls f(x). Feed it a number and watch what comes out.

right_triangle_geometry.questionBridge: Ahmes pulls the knotted rope taut. Two sides fix the third, and the right angle keeps its promise.

trigonometry_basics.questionBridge: Hipparchus lowers his instrument. Angles and sides are bound together. Use one to find the other.

linear_inequalities.questionBridge: William weighs the manifest. The answer isn't one number. It's a range. Find where the limit lies.

systems_of_linear_equations.questionBridge: Liu Hui lays the counting rods in rows. Two unknowns, two conditions. Make them agree at once.

exponent_rules.questionBridge: Jacob watches the powers stack. Repeated multiplication has its own rules. Use them.

polynomials.questionBridge: Omar sketches the curve in the margin. Many terms, one expression. Read what it does.

factoring_polynomials.questionBridge: François treats every expression as a cipher. It was built from factors. Take it apart.

radical_expressions.questionBridge: Hippasus lowers his voice. Some numbers hide under the root sign. Handle them exactly.

quadratic_equations.questionBridge: Muhammad draws the square. Find the side that makes it whole.

descriptive_statistics.questionBridge: Florence sets down her pen. What does the data actually say?

basic_probability.questionBridge: Cardano taps the table. Before you wager, you need the true odds.
basic_probability.diceFrame: Cardano shakes the dice in his cupped hands and slams them onto the table.
basic_probability.spinnerFrame: Cardano flicks the pointer into a blur and dares you to call where it stops.

exponential_functions.questionBridge: John traces the doubling. Growth that feeds on itself. Track how fast it runs.

logarithmic_functions.questionBridge: John slides his great table across the desk. To undo a power, ask what exponent got you there.

sequences_series.questionBridge: Carl reaches for his slate. There is a pattern in the terms. Find it before you count.

lines_angles.questionBridge: Euclid draws crossing lines in the sand. Every angle here is related to another. Reason it out.

triangles_congruence.questionBridge: Euclid lays the triangles side by side. Show they are the same: every side, every angle.

circles_geometry.questionBridge: Archimedes traces the perfect curve. Every point is the same distance from the center. Build from that.

area_volume.questionBridge: Archimedes submerges the crown. Space and substance. Measure what's inside.

geometric_transformations.questionBridge: Maurits studies the tiles. Slide it, flip it, turn it, and ask what stays the same.

rational_expressions.questionBridge: Gottfried sharpens his quill. A fraction of expressions. Treat the top and bottom with equal rigor.

complex_numbers.questionBridge: Rafael refuses to discard the impossible number. Follow the rules anyway. They still hold.

vectors.questionBridge: James reaches for his arrows. Magnitude and direction both matter here.

matrices.questionBridge: Arthur arranges the numbers into a grid. The grid itself obeys algebra. Work it row by column.

conic_sections.questionBridge: Johannes checks the data once more. Slice the cone. The curve tells you which cut was made.

probability_distributions.questionBridge: Abraham tallies a thousand trials. Chance has a shape. Read the whole distribution.

applications_of_derivatives.questionBridge: Isaac watches the apple fall. How fast, and exactly when: the rate of change answers both.

integrals.questionBridge: Archimedes slices the shape into slivers thinner than hairs. Add up the small pieces. The total is exact.

applications_of_integrals.questionBridge: John checks the cables one more time. Accumulate the small pieces. The whole must hold.

inferential_statistics.questionBridge: Ronald walks the muddy plots at dawn. One sample proves nothing. Test what the evidence can bear.

number_properties.questionBridge: Srinivasa sees the pattern before anyone else. Look at what these numbers share.

measurement_units.questionBridge: Antoine weighs everything twice. Convert precisely. The measurement must be exact.

algebraic_manipulation.questionBridge: Muhammad calls it al-jabr, the restoring. Rearrange the symbols. Keep both sides in balance.

representation_translation.questionBridge: René sees the same truth in a graph, a table, an equation. Translate between them.

act_strategy.settingLine: A kitchen table, Saturday morning, present day
act_strategy.questionBridge: Maya starts the timer. Every second is a choice. Pick your approach and commit.

coordinate_geometry.questionBridge: Descartes watches a fly on the ceiling. Two numbers are enough to name any point.

absolute_value.questionBridge: Argand draws the number line. Distance from zero: direction doesn't matter here.

integer_operations.questionBridge: Brahmagupta argues that debts are real numbers. Work with what you have.

polynomial_operations.questionBridge: Cardano combines the terms carefully. Like with like. Simplify until it's clean.

percent_ratio.questionBridge: Pacioli checks the ledger. Out of every hundred: what portion is this?

data_interpretation.questionBridge: Snow marks each case on the map. The data is telling a story. Read it.

combinatorics.questionBridge: Pascal writes to Fermat. How many ways can this happen? Count them all.
